title: ADR-0072 S7: doc the system_failure_halt StopReason + failure_policy in runtime-reference; refine the never_stop rule (task->never-stop; system->halt+escalate) in AGENTS.md — OPERATOR NOD REQUIRED for the rule change
summary: The deterministic floor shipped (d531fc32) with ADR-0072 as the design of record. S7 closes the doc loop: (1) add StopReason 'system_failure_halt' (rc 4) + the failure_policy block to docs/operations/runtime-reference.md's StopReason/env tables; (2) refine the HIGHEST standing rule never_stop_queue_inject_inbox in AGENTS.md to state the system-vs-task distinction. The ADR already documents the reconciliation; AGENTS.md is a governance doc so the rule-text edit needs the operator's explicit nod first.
root_cause: runtime-reference.md's StopReason table predates system_failure_halt; the never_stop rule in AGENTS.md reads as an absolute ('NEVER stop the loop') but ADR-0072 refines it (system-level failures halt while still injecting the queue). Doc drift + a governance rule that now needs a scoped exception documented.
fix: (1) runtime-reference.md: add rows for StopReason system_failure_halt (rc 4, non-resumable-until-diagnosed) and the failure_policy policy block (categories, floor, thresholds). (2) AGENTS.md: refine never_stop to 'task-level failure -> never stop (retry/defer/quarantine, queue via inbox); system-level failure (verdict-incoherence, infra-systemic, non-progress) -> halt + escalate + auto-file P0 pipeline-repair (the queue is still injected)'. (3) Update the memory feedback rule never_stop_queue_inject_inbox with the same refinement. Pure docs otherwise.
acceptance: runtime-reference StopReason table includes system_failure_halt (rc 4) and failure_policy documented.
AGENTS.md never_stop rule states the task-vs-system distinction (AFTER operator nod).
No behavior change; pure-docs (skip simplify/review per policy).
notes: GOVERNANCE FLAG: the never_stop rule is a HIGHEST standing rule. ADR-0072 keeps it consistent (queue still injected, only the loop halts) but the AGENTS.md text edit should be confirmed with the operator before landing. Everything else in S7 is safe pure-docs.
